headline: Transcript: The Bethlehem Project — Being young in Trump’s America
summary: Sonja Hutson and Ian Hodgson talk to Brady Carvajal, Polina Dorman and others
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Swamp Notes podcast episode: ‘The Bethlehem Project: Being young in Trump’s America’

Ian HodgsonAll right. We’re walking up to Liberty High School in Bethlehem. It’s one of the city’s two high schools.

Sonja HutsonRecently, my colleague Ian Hodgson and I went back to high school.

Ian HodgsonIt’s this sort of like beautiful beige bricks. I mean, it’s really ornate, like Corinthian columns out front.

Sonja HutsonIt was kind of ridiculously nice for a public school building. But inside, in a lot of ways, it looks like a classic American high school.

Hordes of students streaming through the hallways, a kind of cacophony of people talking to their friends, joking around. Being there really reminded me of my time in high school. You’re on the precipice of this massive change in your life, becoming an adult and going out into the world.

But a lot has also changed since I was a teenager in the early 2010s. The US is more polarised. There’s a lot of econ
